characters.name: Clyde Oberholt
characters.description: Clyde Oberholtis a young, ambitious consultant and a major suck-up, he is the perennial court jester of his team, always playing practical jokes and trying to get dirt on his co-workers. As a graduate of Wharton, specialized in marketing and spin, his mind-numbing statistical models are so complex no one really knows what they mean. Clyde was assigned toMarty's pod atGalweather & Stearn, but disappointed with how things were going there, he made a major move to acompeting firmto work for Marty's ruthless, unhingedex-wife.[1]

characters.name: Courtney Pelios
characters.description: Courtney Peliosis the daughter ofMarco Pelios, more commonly known as the Rainmaker. She works at a bar off of Sunset Boulevard. When she sleeps with Marty Kaan and he realizes who she is, he tells her to say hello to her father for him.

characters.name: Doug Guggenheim
characters.description: PreviouslyGalweather & Stearn Doug Guggenheimis a graduate of Harvard Business School, and member ofMarty's team atGalweather. Doug is a brilliant numbers guy, a quintessential math geek with mad analytical skills. He tends to be a little slow catching up with everyone else, however he is very good at what he does.

characters.name: Julianne Hotschragar
characters.description: Julianne Hofschrager was the interim CEO ofGalweather & Stearn,who took on the job after she wasted $100 million in a failed run for governor. She has a self stated reputation for "turning around small internet companies". In season three, she was replaced bythe Rainmaker. Hofschrager became CEO afterthe Rainmakerbecame embroiled in a sexual harassment scandal - brought to attention byJeanniein order to prevent a hostile takeover of the company -  and was forced to resign. She proclaimed that there would be a new era at the company, namely one of inclusion and gender equality. Upon her induction, she offered Jeannie a promotion that was ultimately nothing more than a title in hopes of appeasing her and convincing her not to file a class action lawsuit against G&S. She frequently threatened Marty Kaan and inadvertently pushed him to start up his own firm.

characters.name: Kelsey
characters.description: KelseyisClyde's ex-girlfriend and one of the employees at the app start-up company that once shared an office withKaan & Associates. She and Clyde eventually worked together to launch an app named Housecallz and later began dating. When Clyde first approached her in the office, she brushed off his advances. Later, she approached him for help with the business aspects of developing Housecallz, an app that she coded to be "Uber but for doctors" using GPS technology. Following Doug's divorce, Clyde got him involved with the development of the app as well. Kelsey, along with Clyde, enjoyed making fun of Doug a little bit, and it seemed like she and Clyde were growing closer. At the "One Child, One Spirit" charity event, Clyde attempted to ask her on a date, but she bluntly rejected him. While working on the app with Doug, the two connected over their shared interests, which led to a sexual encounter. This became a regular arrangement. Eventually, Doug felt guilty about this hurting Clyde, and he told Kelsey about this and tried to give her a better impression of Clyde. Due to this, they agreed to put an end to their arrangement.

characters.description: Klare wasMarty Kaan's new girlfriend. She is a vegan preacher and Instagram model who stayed with Marty for a brief time. Klare andMarty Klare andMartyin the morning

characters.name: Malcolm Kaan
characters.description: Malcolm Kaan is a recurring fictional character in Showtime'sHouse of Lies. He appears inseason two. Malcolm is a member of the Kaan family, son ofJeremiah, brother ofMartyand uncle ofRoscoe. He also had a huge crush onMonicawho was the wife of his brother at that time. Later he has a big opportunity to romance with her.

characters.name: Marty Kaan
characters.description: Marty Kaanis the lead character - an extraordinary but unscrupulous management consultant, gifted at identifying, and exploiting, the weaknesses of clients to get them to sign on the dotted line.  He is offensive, egotistical, narcissistic, womanizing, crafty, and very "un-PC," all of which make him very good at his job.[1] Marty was a Junior Partner atGalweather & Stearn, but he finally found the courage to branch out and start hisown firm. Although initially, his pod was wary, they eventually joined him at Kaan & Associates. When his mother committed suicide, he was left with three remaining family members with whom he has had tumultuous relationships. His gender non-conforming sonRoscoewas the subject of a custody battle with hisex-wife; he fought with hisbrotherover police brutality and racism; and his father kept his Parkinson's disease diagnosis from him. Despite this, his family are important to him and consequently his work performance frequently suffered at Galweather's.

characters.name: Monica Talbot
characters.description: Monica Talbot or ex-Monica Kaan is a hotshot consultant withKinsley-Johnson, andMarty'sconstant professional nemesis. Monica is also Marty's ex-wife and the mother of their sonRoscoe. Since Roscoe moved back in with his father full-time, Monica is able to devote all her considerable energies to her career. Marty often refers to her as a sociopath and when they met, they often end up with angry sexual activities together.[1] Monica andMartyon the bed

characters.name: Sarah Guggenheim
characters.description: Sarah Diamondis the ex-wife ofDoug Guggenheim. She met him over a dating service that all the members of the pod were invited to join, and she had a high compatibility percentage with Doug. Although she didn't have a profile picture, they went on a date and instantly hit it off.Jeannieencountered her in a hotel room with Doug after she returned from her own disastrous date. She metMartyandClydeat a corporate getaway she had snuck into hosted byJulianne Hofschrager, again in a compromising position. After seeing how disrespectfully Doug was treated by even his closest friends, she encouraged him to stand up for himself. While there, Clyde unsuccessfully hit on her and sparked an argument with Doug. While the pod was in Vegas for a client, she and Doug went out to a restaurant. There, she found a ring in her dessert and tearfully accepted Doug's proposal. It was only a day later, at the wedding ceremony, that she came clean and admitted that she had put the ring there herself to manipulate him into a marriage. Relieved that the ring was paid for, he went ahead with it and married her happily.
